cards.realSize: plasma major radius 0.93 m; device several metres tall
cards.blurb: A spherical tokamak - a cored-apple-shaped plasma that reaches high pressure with less magnetic field than a conventional tokamak.

cards.realSize: plasma major radius 6.2 m; machine roughly 30 m tall
cards.blurb: The world's largest tokamak, under construction in southern France. Designed to produce 500 MW of fusion power from 50 MW of heating.
Columbia's research tokamak, used to study how to control plasma instabilities with feedback from magnetic sensors and active coils.

cards.realSize: compact device, a few metres long
cards.blurb: A field-reversed configuration: the plasma generates much of its own confining field, promising small, simple fusion devices.

cards.realSize: engine concept roughly 1-2 m in diameter
cards.blurb: A fusion rocket engine concept based on the PFRC, designed to fly missions like a Pluto orbiter with power and thrust from one compact core.
